---
title: "Glossary"
citation: false
format:
  html:
    toc-depth: 3
  pdf: default
---

This glossary is a non-exhaustive source for definitions of key terms and concepts related to the Analytics Engine and its associated tools and applications. This page includes detailed explanations of data products, use cases, analytical tools, and methodologies used in climate data analysis to support a user's understanding and application of the information in the Analytics Engine. This glossary is designed to complement existing extensive meteorological glossaries, such as the American Meteorological Society's [Glossary of Meteorology](https://www.ametsoc.org/index.cfm/ams/publications/glossary-of-meteorology/), with specific terminologies that are focused on the Analytics Engine.

{{< include _glossary-generated.qmd >}}

## References

- American Meteorological Society. *Glossary of Meteorology.* <https://glossary.ametsoc.org/>
- Cal-Adapt Analytics Engine. *About Climate Projections and Models.* <https://analytics.cal-adapt.org/guidance/about_climate_projections_and_models>
- CF Conventions. *NetCDF Climate and Forecast Metadata Conventions.* <https://cfconventions.org/>
- Coles, S. (2001). *An Introduction to Statistical Modeling of Extreme Values.* Springer.
- Eyring, V., et al. (2016). Overview of the Coupled Model Intercomparison Project Phase 6 (CMIP6). *Geoscientific Model Development*, 9, 1937–1958.
- Gutiérrez, J.M., et al. (2021). *IPCC AR6 Interactive Atlas.* In: Climate Change 2021: The Physical Science Basis. <https://interactive-atlas.ipcc.ch/>
- Intergovernmental Panel on Climate Change (IPCC). *Sixth Assessment Report.* <https://www.ipcc.ch/report/sixth-assessment-report-cycle/>
- Lafferty, D.C., et al. (2023). *LOCA2-Hybrid Downscaled Climate Projections for California.* <https://loca.ucsd.edu/>
- National Renewable Energy Laboratory. *National Solar Radiation Database — Typical Meteorological Year.* <https://nsrdb.nrel.gov/data-sets/tmy>
- O'Neill, B.C., et al. (2016). The Scenario Model Intercomparison Project (ScenarioMIP) for CMIP6. *Geoscientific Model Development*, 9, 3461–3482.
- Zarr Development Team. *Zarr: An implementation of chunked, compressed, N-dimensional arrays.* <https://zarr.dev/>
